MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On Plaintiffs Claim For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Mar-24-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Bill D. Fountain of HAWKINS PARNELL THACKSTON & YOUNG (415-766-3202) appearing for defendant / moving party John Crane Inc.
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties James Angus and Juana Angus

RULING:
DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON PLAINTIFFS' CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Specifically, defendant failed to demonstrate that Mr. Angus was not exposed to John Crane packing in or after 1970, the date by which defendant concedes it learned of the hazards of asbestos. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is denied under California law. This is without prejudice to defendant John Crane Inc. raising the choice of law issue with the trial judge that Virginia law should apply to the issue of punitive damage. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Cathy Meyer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11596 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)
